title: Manager im Bereich SAP Project System (PS) (all genders)
required_skills: 5+ years of SAP consulting or inhouse consulting experience (Automotive/Manufacturing focus)
SAP PS module expertise (implementation and application)
Integration knowledge across FI/CO, SD, MM, PP or PM modules
Process modelling and optimisation in SAP context
bonus_skills: SAP PPM or SAP CPM experience
matched_skills: Project coordination and stakeholder management (HSBC, Hapag-Lloyd)
Presentation and reporting for executive audiences (HSBC C-suite, Hapag-Lloyd sprint reviews)
Structured analytical working style (budget dashboards, KPI frameworks)
missing_skills: SAP FI/CO, SD, MM, PP or PM module knowledge
Process modelling expertise in SAP environment
core_responsibilities: Advise clients on SAP PS process implementation and optimisation
Integration consulting between business and IT within SAP ecosystem
Assess material flows in manufacturing: MM, PP, SD processes
Apply new technologies including S/4HANA and agile PM tools
Flexibly take advisory roles across assessment, design, implementation, testing, rollout and support phases
recommended_emphasis: HSBC: Programme coordination across 6 workstreams — closest analogue to cross-module SAP coordination
Executive reporting and stakeholder communication — relevant to client advisory dimension
Analytical mindset demonstrated through budget variance dashboards and KPI framework design
summary: Boru scores 18.2 (poor match) as this is a specialist SAP PS consulting role requiring deep hands-on SAP module experience and automotive/manufacturing industry knowledge — both entirely absent from the CV. The 5-year SAP consulting requirement is a hard filter. General PM and coordination skills do not compensate for the technical SAP depth this role demands. Application is not recommended.
